forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+project: kv-prefix-reuse
+title: 'Fork-restore: honor session/fork as a zero-rollback forward-only KV restore on the streaming path'
+---
+## What
+Make llama-agent's streaming decode path honor fork lineage as a DETERMINISTIC zero-rollback restore — the reliable prefix-caching primitive for a recurrent/hybrid model (Qwen), where arbitrary mid-sequence rollback is structurally impossible and the generic LCP+rollback heuristic can never be reliable.
+
+Root cause (verified): the validators layer correctly primes + forks (`fleet.rs:241,258`, `pool.rs submit_primed/submit_forked`, `acp/session_fork.rs`), but `process_streaming_request_sync` IGNORES the fork alias — it re-renders the child's full prompt and runs the generic `find_best_prefix_match` LCP scan (`queue.rs:2371-2401,2598-2642`), which the 64-token `n_rs_seq` cap then defeats. So `session/fork` has no teeth on the local qwen path and degrades to N independent pinned full-prompt sessions (observed in calcutron-qwen: every turn `no usable cached prefix … processing full prompt`, 0 reuse).
+
+Implement (in `crates/llama-agent/src`, primarily `queue.rs` streaming path):
+- FIRST diagnose precisely why the child's own fork-alias entry (parent state_bytes + parent fingerprint, `queue.rs:407-411`) isn't already selected at rollback 0 by the (now rollback-aware) `find_best_prefix_match`. Likely the child turn's rendered prompt does not extend the parent at the token level, or the alias isn't consulted as a fork. Record findings as a comment.
+- When a request's session was created via `session/fork` (parent known), treat the child's prompt as parent_prompt + appended suffix: restore the parent's aliased KV state (`set_state_data`) and prefill ONLY the appended suffix as a forward extension — NO LCP scan, NO `seq_rm`/trim, NO rollback. This is recurrent-safe by construction (forward-only) and independent of `n_rs_seq`.
+- Carry the MTP draft state the same way (the draft rides with the parent; extend it forward too) so `skipping MTP this turn` stays 0.
+- Fall back to the existing LCP path only when there is no fork parent. Depends on the token-prefix-stability guarantee (card 309wyrm) — the parent tokens MUST be an exact prefix of the child tokens.
+- General capability: keyed on ACP `session/fork` lineage, NOT on the review tool. Any forking client benefits.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A forked child turn restores the parent KV and prefills only the appended suffix (rollback 0); no `seq_rm`/trim and no LCP scan on the fork path.
+- [ ] On the recurrent qwen model, a fork chain reuses the parent prefix regardless of suffix length (no 64-token ceiling on the fork path).
+- [ ] MTP retained on fork turns (`skipping MTP this turn` does not fire).
+- [ ] Non-forked sessions still use the existing LCP path unchanged.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Gated real-model integration test (extend `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/kv_prefix_reuse_recurrent.rs`) using an ACTUAL `session/fork` (not a hand-built donor): prime a parent, fork >=2 children with suffixes > 64 tokens, assert each child reuses the full parent prefix with zero rollback and `skipping MTP this turn` fires 0 times. RED before this card (children full-reprocess), GREEN after.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p llama-agent --lib` green; clippy -D warnings clean; gated test passes on GPU.
+
+## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges currently dropped by a known bug): 309wyrm (token-prefix stability). Builds on the shipped selector fix zckz9va + model wiring x3181nx.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the fork-chain real-model assertion first (RED), diagnose, implement fork-restore, GREEN.
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,34 +0,0 @@
----
-assignees:
-- claude-code
-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: a180
-project: kv-prefix-reuse
-title: 'Fork-restore: honor session/fork as a zero-rollback forward-only KV restore on the streaming path'
----
-## What
-Make llama-agent's streaming decode path honor fork lineage as a DETERMINISTIC zero-rollback restore — the reliable prefix-caching primitive for a recurrent/hybrid model (Qwen), where arbitrary mid-sequence rollback is structurally impossible and the generic LCP+rollback heuristic can never be reliable.
-
-Root cause (verified): the validators layer correctly primes + forks (`fleet.rs:241,258`, `pool.rs submit_primed/submit_forked`, `acp/session_fork.rs`), but `process_streaming_request_sync` IGNORES the fork alias — it re-renders the child's full prompt and runs the generic `find_best_prefix_match` LCP scan (`queue.rs:2371-2401,2598-2642`), which the 64-token `n_rs_seq` cap then defeats. So `session/fork` has no teeth on the local qwen path and degrades to N independent pinned full-prompt sessions (observed in calcutron-qwen: every turn `no usable cached prefix … processing full prompt`, 0 reuse).
-
-Implement (in `crates/llama-agent/src`, primarily `queue.rs` streaming path):
-- FIRST diagnose precisely why the child's own fork-alias entry (parent state_bytes + parent fingerprint, `queue.rs:407-411`) isn't already selected at rollback 0 by the (now rollback-aware) `find_best_prefix_match`. Likely the child turn's rendered prompt does not extend the parent at the token level, or the alias isn't consulted as a fork. Record findings as a comment.
-- When a request's session was created via `session/fork` (parent known), treat the child's prompt as parent_prompt + appended suffix: restore the parent's aliased KV state (`set_state_data`) and prefill ONLY the appended suffix as a forward extension — NO LCP scan, NO `seq_rm`/trim, NO rollback. This is recurrent-safe by construction (forward-only) and independent of `n_rs_seq`.
-- Carry the MTP draft state the same way (the draft rides with the parent; extend it forward too) so `skipping MTP this turn` stays 0.
-- Fall back to the existing LCP path only when there is no fork parent. Depends on the token-prefix-stability guarantee (card 309wyrm) — the parent tokens MUST be an exact prefix of the child tokens.
-- General capability: keyed on ACP `session/fork` lineage, NOT on the review tool. Any forking client benefits.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A forked child turn restores the parent KV and prefills only the appended suffix (rollback 0); no `seq_rm`/trim and no LCP scan on the fork path.
-- [ ] On the recurrent qwen model, a fork chain reuses the parent prefix regardless of suffix length (no 64-token ceiling on the fork path).
-- [ ] MTP retained on fork turns (`skipping MTP this turn` does not fire).
-- [ ] Non-forked sessions still use the existing LCP path unchanged.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Gated real-model integration test (extend `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/kv_prefix_reuse_recurrent.rs`) using an ACTUAL `session/fork` (not a hand-built donor): prime a parent, fork >=2 children with suffixes > 64 tokens, assert each child reuses the full parent prefix with zero rollback and `skipping MTP this turn` fires 0 times. RED before this card (children full-reprocess), GREEN after.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p llama-agent --lib` green; clippy -D warnings clean; gated test passes on GPU.
-
-## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges currently dropped by a known bug): 309wyrm (token-prefix stability). Builds on the shipped selector fix zckz9va + model wiring x3181nx.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the fork-chain real-model assertion first (RED), diagnose, implement fork-restore, GREEN.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,31 +4,34 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a180
 project: kv-prefix-reuse
-title: 'Fork-restore: honor session/fork as a zero-rollback forward-only KV restore on the streaming path'
+title: Verify fork-chain reuse end-to-end; add only the minimal restore code the RED test proves necessary
 ---
 ## What
-Make llama-agent's streaming decode path honor fork lineage as a DETERMINISTIC zero-rollback restore — the reliable prefix-caching primitive for a recurrent/hybrid model (Qwen), where arbitrary mid-sequence rollback is structurally impossible and the generic LCP+rollback heuristic can never be reliable.
+TEST-FIRST, de-risked. Adversarial review found the existing rollback-aware selector (zckz9va) likely ALREADY performs a zero-rollback fork restore once token-prefix stability (309wyrm) holds: a forked child's own cache entry is keyed by the child id and carries the parent's state + the parent's prompt-token fingerprint (`SessionStateStore::fork`, `queue.rs:407-411,422-454`). If the child tokens extend the parent tokens, `find_best_prefix_match(child_id, child_tokens)` yields `lcp == donor_len`, `rollback == 0` → passes the ≤64 gate → wins → `streaming_reuse_decision` returns `Some(donor_len)` → trim clears an empty range → forward-only restore. So this card may collapse to "regression test + docs."

-Root cause (verified): the validators layer correctly primes + forks (`fleet.rs:241,258`, `pool.rs submit_primed/submit_forked`, `acp/session_fork.rs`), but `process_streaming_request_sync` IGNORES the fork alias — it re-renders the child's full prompt and runs the generic `find_best_prefix_match` LCP scan (`queue.rs:2371-2401,2598-2642`), which the 64-token `n_rs_seq` cap then defeats. So `session/fork` has no teeth on the local qwen path and degrades to N independent pinned full-prompt sessions (observed in calcutron-qwen: every turn `no usable cached prefix … processing full prompt`, 0 reuse).
+DO THIS, in order:
+1. Write the RED real-model fork-chain test (below) and run it with ONLY 309wyrm + the shipped selector landed.
+2. If GREEN: this card is the test + documentation of the mechanism. Done. Do NOT add a redundant restore path.
+3. If still RED: diagnose with the live-path evidence and add the MINIMAL change needed, choosing explicitly:
+   - (a) If the child's own aliased entry isn't being selected though tokens extend the parent → fix selection/aliasing.
+   - (b) If decode-time lineage is genuinely needed → thread the parent session id through `GenerationRequest` (`crates/llama-agent/src/types/generation.rs`) and `QueuedRequest` (`queue.rs:~1158`) the SAME way `pin_on_save` is threaded via ACP `_meta` (no `parent_session_id` field exists today — that wiring is the work), and prefer the named parent in selection. Only build this if the test proves it necessary.

-Implement (in `crates/llama-agent/src`, primarily `queue.rs` streaming path):
-- FIRST diagnose precisely why the child's own fork-alias entry (parent state_bytes + parent fingerprint, `queue.rs:407-411`) isn't already selected at rollback 0 by the (now rollback-aware) `find_best_prefix_match`. Likely the child turn's rendered prompt does not extend the parent at the token level, or the alias isn't consulted as a fork. Record findings as a comment.
-- When a request's session was created via `session/fork` (parent known), treat the child's prompt as parent_prompt + appended suffix: restore the parent's aliased KV state (`set_state_data`) and prefill ONLY the appended suffix as a forward extension — NO LCP scan, NO `seq_rm`/trim, NO rollback. This is recurrent-safe by construction (forward-only) and independent of `n_rs_seq`.
-- Carry the MTP draft state the same way (the draft rides with the parent; extend it forward too) so `skipping MTP this turn` stays 0.
-- Fall back to the existing LCP path only when there is no fork parent. Depends on the token-prefix-stability guarantee (card 309wyrm) — the parent tokens MUST be an exact prefix of the child tokens.
-- General capability: keyed on ACP `session/fork` lineage, NOT on the review tool. Any forking client benefits.
+Edge case to cover regardless: empty/near-empty suffix. `streaming_reuse_decision` returns `None` when `lcp >= new_len` (`queue.rs` ~2895) → full reprocess. A forked child whose suffix renders to zero new tokens must be handled as "fully cached, decode nothing new," not a cold reprocess.
+
+General capability — keyed on fork lineage / token-prefix extension, NOT the review tool.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A forked child turn restores the parent KV and prefills only the appended suffix (rollback 0); no `seq_rm`/trim and no LCP scan on the fork path.
-- [ ] On the recurrent qwen model, a fork chain reuses the parent prefix regardless of suffix length (no 64-token ceiling on the fork path).
-- [ ] MTP retained on fork turns (`skipping MTP this turn` does not fire).
-- [ ] Non-forked sessions still use the existing LCP path unchanged.
+- [ ] Gated real-model fork-chain test passes: prime a parent, fork ≥2 children with suffixes > 64 tokens via ACTUAL `session/fork`, each child reuses the full parent prefix at rollback 0 (no `seq_rm` failure, no full reprocess).
+- [ ] Non-forked sessions unchanged (LCP path intact).
+- [ ] Empty/near-empty suffix on the fork path does not cause a cold reprocess.
+- [ ] Whatever code (if any) was added is the minimum the RED test required; if none was needed, that is documented.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Gated real-model integration test (extend `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/kv_prefix_reuse_recurrent.rs`) using an ACTUAL `session/fork` (not a hand-built donor): prime a parent, fork >=2 children with suffixes > 64 tokens, assert each child reuses the full parent prefix with zero rollback and `skipping MTP this turn` fires 0 times. RED before this card (children full-reprocess), GREEN after.
+- [ ] Extend `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/kv_prefix_reuse_recurrent.rs` to use real `session/fork` (not hand-built donors). RED before 309wyrm/this card, GREEN after.
+- [ ] MTP on fork turns (corrected, NON-over-strict AC): assert `skipping MTP this turn` does NOT fire on fork turns *when the prime turn executed MTP*. Note explicitly: a prime that did not run MTP saves no draft, so the first fork legitimately cold-starts the draft — do not assert 0 skips in that case. (`apply_draft_kv_state` aliases parent `draft_state_bytes` at `queue.rs:443`; trim to `offset==donor_len` is a 0-distance trim that succeeds only if the parent draft length == donor_len.)
 - [ ] `cargo test -p llama-agent --lib` green; clippy -D warnings clean; gated test passes on GPU.

-## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges currently dropped by a known bug): 309wyrm (token-prefix stability). Builds on the shipped selector fix zckz9va + model wiring x3181nx.
+## Depends on (prose — kanban depends_on edges dropped by a known bug): 309wyrm (token-prefix stability) MUST land first — it may be the entire fix. Builds on shipped zckz9va + x3181nx.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the fork-chain real-model assertion first (RED), diagnose, implement fork-restore, GREEN.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — RED fork-chain test first; land 309wyrm; re-run; add minimal code ONLY if still RED.
\ No newline at end of file
